summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Cricket App backend APIs and code structure verification. Backend fully functional with all cricket data endpoints working. Frontend is React Native Expo app - code structure verified but web testing limited due to mobile-first architecture.

integration_issues.issue: React Native Expo app not accessible via web browser - designed for mobile devices
integration_issues.note: This is expected behavior for React Native apps

passed_tests: Backend root endpoint /api/ returns Hello World
Backend live matches endpoint working (5 matches found)
Backend recent matches endpoint working (47 matches found)
Backend upcoming matches endpoint working (9 matches found)
Backend commentary endpoint working with real match IDs
Backend API key rotation system implemented
index.tsx extractMatches creates proper Match objects with matchId, teams[], status, category fields
Category filter uses match.category instead of match.matchFormat
Navigation uses matchId instead of id for routing
Header component onUnlockPro prop is optional
ErrorScreen supports both onGoBack and onRetry props
FloatingScoreboard has increased size (minWidth 230, proper font sizes)
Match type interface has proper matchId, teams, status fields
Backend URL configuration correct in api.ts
TypeMatches structure validation in API responses
